reference_standards: BS 8000:12 (Workmanship on building sites - Decorative wallcoverings and painting)
City & Guilds 6707 Level 2 Diploma in Painting and Decorating
CSCS Painter and Decorator occupational standard

tasks.title: Surface Assessment
tasks.instruction: Photograph a wall with visible defects. Identify each defect (cracks, damp, flaking, holes). Describe correct preparation method for each.

checks: Identifies any hazardous materials (e.g. potential lead paint on pre-1960s surfaces, asbestos textures)
Mentions appropriate PPE for preparation work (dust mask for sanding, goggles)
All visible defects correctly identified and named
Cracks: correctly categorised (hairline vs structural) with appropriate remedy (fill and sand for hairline, investigate cause for structural per BS 6150)
Damp: identified and correct advice given (treat cause before painting, not just paint over — BS 6150 clause on moisture)
Flaking paint: correct prep described (scrape back to firm edge, sand smooth, prime bare areas)
Holes/damaged plaster: correct filler type selected (lightweight filler for small holes, bonding plaster for large areas)
Correct primer selected for substrate (acrylic primer for new plaster, alkali-resistant primer for cement, stain block for water stains)
All defects identified (none missed)
Preparation methods are appropriate and in correct order
Explanation is clear and demonstrates understanding of why each step matters

tasks.title: Masking & Protection
tasks.instruction: Mask off a window frame, skirting board, and light switch. Lay drop sheets on floor.

checks: Light switch: power isolated or switch plate removed safely before masking
Drop sheets laid to prevent slip hazard (secured, not loose)
No trailing tape or materials creating trip hazard
Masking tape applied in long continuous strips (not short patches)
Tape pressed firmly to edge with finger or tool (no lifting/bubbling — prevents paint bleed)
Window frame: tape follows frame edge precisely, no gaps between tape and frame
Skirting board: tape along top edge, straight and tight
Light switch: fully covered or plate removed and edges masked
Drop sheets cover full working area including 300mm+ beyond work zone (per BS 8000:12 protection requirements)
Drop sheets secured at edges (taped or weighted)
All surfaces requiring protection are fully masked
No gaps in tape lines
Tape lines are straight and clean
Floor fully protected
Overall preparation looks professional and thorough

tasks.title: Cutting In
tasks.instruction: Freehand (no tape) cut in paint along ceiling line, internal corner, and around a door frame.

checks: Stable footing (step ladder or platform used correctly if working at height)
Paint tin/kettle held securely (not balanced precariously)
Area below protected
Brush selection: angled cutting-in brush (50-63mm) used — correct tool for the job
Brush held correctly on narrow edge (not flat side) for control (per professional practice)
Brush loaded correctly: dipped 1/3 into paint, excess removed on kettle edge (not overloaded)
Steady hand movement along ceiling line — brush drawn in long smooth strokes, not dabbed
Consistent pressure applied throughout stroke
Internal corner: brush worked into corner without overloading
Door frame: clean line maintained around architrave edge
Paint feathered out from cut line to blend with roller area (no hard edge buildup)
Ceiling line is straight and consistent (max 1-2mm deviation per BS 8000:12 workmanship standards)
No paint on ceiling surface
No paint on door frame/architrave
Corner line clean and consistent
Coverage even at edges — no thin spots or runs
Cut-in area blends naturally (no visible boundary where roller will meet)

tasks.title: Roll a Wall Section
tasks.instruction: Load roller from tray, apply paint in W-pattern across 2m² section, lay off with even vertical strokes, blend into cut-in edges.

checks: Paint tray on stable surface (not on top of ladder or precarious position)
Floor protection in place
No overreaching or unsafe ladder use
Roller loaded evenly from tray — rolled back and forth on ramp to distribute paint (not just dipped and applied)
First pass: W or M pattern to distribute paint across section (per professional standard technique)
Second pass: even vertical strokes to lay off and eliminate roller marks
Consistent pressure — not pressing too hard (starving roller) or too light (drips)
Working from top to bottom of section
Maintaining a wet edge throughout (avoiding lap marks from dried edges)
Blending into cut-in edges — roller brought close but not touching ceiling/trim
Reload frequency appropriate — roller reloaded before going dry
Even coverage across entire section — no thin spots or missed areas
No drips or runs (per BS 6150 finish quality)
Roller marks consistent direction (vertical lay-off)
Consistent sheen/finish across section
Blends seamlessly into cut-in edges
No spattering on protected surfaces

tasks.title: Final Inspection
tasks.instruction: Photograph completed wall from four positions: straight-on overall, angled with raking light, close-up of ceiling line, shot showing masking removed.

checks: All masking tape removed carefully (peeled at 45° angle)
Work area cleaned and tidy (brushes/rollers cleaned or disposed, drop sheets folded)
No paint contamination on non-work surfaces
Photos taken from all four specified angles (demonstrates thorough self-inspection habit)
Raking light photo shows surface quality (reveals any roller marks, drips, or unevenness)
Close-up shows quality of cutting-in line detail
Worker demonstrates ability to self-assess finished work
Even coverage across entire wall — no patchiness or colour variation (per BS 6150 opacity requirements)
Clean lines at all edges (ceiling, skirting, door frame, window)
No visible brush or roller marks under raking light
Consistent colour and sheen across wall
Tape removed cleanly — no paint bleed under tape lines
No paint drips, runs, or sags anywhere
Area left clean and tidy — professional finish overall
